channel.title: The Grant Desk - Cross-disciplinary & Social Impact, Worldwide
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Cross-disciplinary & Social Impact, Worldwide.

item.title: King Baudouin Foundation: Ernest Solvay Fund 2026 (Call for Projects)
item.description:
Organisation: Fonds Ernest Solvay, managed with the support of the King Baudouin Foundation

Location: Organisations implementing a project within a 100 km radius of a Solvay industrial, R&I or administrative site

Award: Up to EUR 10,000 per initiative. Funding covers only expenses incurred after the results are announced (mid-December 2026); already-incurred expenses are not reimbursed. No application fee.

Duration: Project implemented after mid-December 2026 announcement

Deadline: 30 Sep 2026

The Ernest Solvay Fund provides financial support for local-impact initiatives in three areas: scientific education (science, technology, engineering and mathematics), planet progress (environmental initiatives) and better life (community well-being). ELIGIBILITY: organisations submitting a project must implement it within a 100 km radius of a Solvay industrial, R&I or administrative site. FUNDING: up to EUR 10,000 per initiative; the Fund covers expenses incurred only after the results are announced (mid-December 2026) and will not reimburse expenses incurred beforehand. APPLY: create or log in to an account at https://candidate.kbs-frb.be and complete the online application form (it can be saved and completed in several stages). Applications are open 15 April 2026 to 30 September 2026; results announced mid-December 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Emergent Ventures (Mercatus Center): Grants for Individuals
item.description:
Organisation: Mercatus Center, George Mason University

Location: Remote; projects and applicants worldwide

Award: Grant size is set per project and is not published (ranges from small fast grants to larger awards). No application fee.

Duration: Varies by project

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Emergent Ventures funds entrepreneurs and thinkers worldwide with highly scalable, zero-to-one ideas for meaningfully improving society. It is aimed at individuals (applicants must be 13 or older) rather than institutions, and international and non-US applicants are eligible; grants are awarded to people around the world. Dedicated support is available for projects focused on India, Africa, the Caribbean or Ukraine. Grant amounts are not disclosed and are set per project. Applications are accepted on a rolling, continuous basis through an online form. The programme launched in 2018 and is administered by Tyler Cowen at the Mercatus Center.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: EU-Japan Fest: Mobility Support 2026 (15 August 2026 Cut-Off)
item.description:
Organisation: EU-Japan Fest Japan Committee (in partnership with Culture Next)

Location: Travel between Japan and European Capitals of Culture / Culture Next member cities

Award: JPY 50,000 to JPY 150,000 per applicant, depending on applicant and travel category. No application fee.

Duration: Travel period April 2026 - September 2026 (applications must be submitted at least 6 weeks prior to travel)

Deadline: 15 Aug 2026

The EU-Japan Fest Japan Committee supports individuals and organisations developing projects between Japan and European Capitals of Culture (past, present or future) as well as other Culture Next member cities. PURPOSE: support travel for research, project planning, and collaboration, building creative networks aimed at realising Japan-Europe arts and culture projects. THEMES (for Japan-Europe collaborative projects): Youth Empowerment; Nature & Green; Diversity (DEI); Social Regeneration. Projects may include participants from other regions (e.g. Asia) joining a Japan-Europe project. TARGET APPLICANTS: members of European Capital of Culture teams; artists and project organisers (including curators, designers, etc.); researchers in culture/arts; social entrepreneurs. TIMING: applications must be submitted at least 6 weeks prior to the travel date; this cut-off covers travel in April 2026 - September 2026. DEADLINE: this card is for the 15 August 2026 cut-off (other cut-offs each have their own card). APPLY via the Culture Next collaboration page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: EU-Japan Fest: Mobility Support 2026-27 (14 February 2027 Cut-Off)
item.description:
Organisation: EU-Japan Fest Japan Committee (in partnership with Culture Next)

Location: Travel between Japan and European Capitals of Culture / Culture Next member cities

Award: JPY 50,000 to JPY 150,000 per applicant, depending on applicant and travel category. No application fee.

Duration: Travel period October 2026 - March 2027 (applications must be submitted at least 6 weeks prior to travel)

Deadline: 14 Feb 2027

The EU-Japan Fest Japan Committee supports individuals and organisations developing projects between Japan and European Capitals of Culture (past, present or future) as well as other Culture Next member cities. PURPOSE: support travel for research, project planning, and collaboration, building creative networks aimed at realising Japan-Europe arts and culture projects. THEMES (for Japan-Europe collaborative projects): Youth Empowerment; Nature & Green; Diversity (DEI); Social Regeneration. Projects may include participants from other regions (e.g. Asia) joining a Japan-Europe project. TARGET APPLICANTS: members of European Capital of Culture teams; artists and project organisers (including curators, designers, etc.); researchers in culture/arts; social entrepreneurs. TIMING: applications must be submitted at least 6 weeks prior to the travel date; this cut-off covers travel in October 2026 - March 2027. DEADLINE: this card is for the 14 February 2027 cut-off (other cut-offs each have their own card). APPLY via the Culture Next collaboration page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Imagination Practice Fund: Year 4
item.description:
Organisation: Collective Imagination Practice Community (Fund Circle, supported by Joseph Rowntree Foundation)

Location: Worldwide

Award: Up to £3,000 per project; approximately 16 collective imagination projects funded

Duration: Activities to complete by Autumn 2026; documentation by December 2026

Deadline: Closed 10 May 2026

Final-year practice fund supporting projects that use collective imagination to change the world by disrupting extractive or divisive narratives and/or nurturing equitable and regenerative imaginaries. Looking for work that pushes the boundaries of collective imagination practice, realises material benefits from it, creates change inside or outside the system, and contributes to the wider Collective Imagination Practice Community via the CIPC Seed Library. Two-stage application: (1) brief initial application by 23:59 BST Sunday 10 May 2026; (2) Community Shortlisting events Tuesday 12 May (10:00 to 12:00 BST) and Thursday 14 May (17:00 to 19:00 BST); (3) shortlist announced Monday 18 May; (4) full application from shortlisted applicants by 23:59 BST Sunday 7 June 2026; (5) successful projects announced Monday 22 June 2026. This year decision-making is shared with the community via the shortlisting events. Apply via the initial application form linked from the Medium post; questions to hello@imaginationpractice.info.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Next Generation Foresight Practitioners (NGFP) Fellowship 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Next Generation Foresight Practitioners (NGFP)

Location: Global (online community plus regional hubs)

Award: $1,000 kick-starter grant + mentorship + global networking + opportunity to win $10,000 grand prize at year-end

Duration: 1 year

Deadline: 26 Jun 2026

Fellowship for emerging changemakers aged 18 to 35 using futures thinking and foresight to drive social and environmental impact. Fellows receive a $1,000 kick-starter grant, mentorship from expert foresight practitioners, capability-building training, access to a 900+ strong global community, regional hubs, and the chance to win a $10,000 grand prize. Supports projects on climate transitions, democracy, emerging technologies, health and other systemic challenges.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Pollination Project: Daily Seed Grants
item.description:
Organisation: The Pollination Project

Location: Anywhere (worldwide)

Award: Up to $500

Duration: Per project

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Daily seed grants for early-stage volunteer-driven projects with social and environmental impact. Open to grassroots changemakers worldwide: individuals, informal groups and small nonprofits. Project budget under $10,000 and organisational budget under $50,000; no paid staff. Applications reviewed monthly; submit before month-end for that month's review.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Awesome Foundation $1,000 Grant
item.description:
Organisation: The Awesome Foundation (network of local chapters)

Location: Worldwide (chapters across many cities)

Award: $1,000 (no strings, no equity)

Duration: One-time, rolling monthly cycle

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Monthly $1,000 micro-grants for awesome ideas. Decentralised network of local chapters around the world; each chapter awards one grant per month. Apply via your nearest chapter on the site. Your idea stays yours, no equity taken.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: 2027 Elevate Prize
item.description:
Organisation: Elevate Prize Foundation

Location: Worldwide (US 501(c)(3) or international equivalent)

Award: $300,000 unrestricted ($250,000 to organisation + $50,000 to the individual)

Duration: 2-year tailored programme + global community

Deadline: Closed 08 May 2026

Nomination-based prize for nonprofit leaders driving meaningful change. Each winner receives $300,000 in unrestricted funding plus a two-year tailored programme with visibility, scaling support and a global community. Eligibility: 18+, serving as Executive Director, CEO, Founder or equivalent of a registered nonprofit (US 501(c)(3) or international equivalent) able to receive philanthropic funds. Ideally for organisations with annual budgets between $2M and $10M (others may be considered based on readiness and impact opportunity).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
